behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename semantics plus CLI parsing/counting. A fake diff can miss real cases where git emits rename statuses (or falls back to add/delete) based on actual repo state. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior token "Renamed files" in a real "git repository" depends on subprocess git output/state, not just one in-process function. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text contains no AI/ML step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo, commit a file, rename it with `git mv`, run the real CLI via subprocess against the repo, and assert report shows +1 renamed, +0 added from rename, +0 deleted from rename.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Crash risk is at real subprocess/encoding boundary with git binary diff output. Mocked text often hides decode/stream edge cases. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration due behavior token "include binary files" requiring real git diff behavior and process output; rejected Agentic/Workflow since no model output quality involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo with a binary file (e.g., random bytes), commit baseline, modify binary, run CLI as subprocess, assert exit code 0 and binary file appears in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This hinges on real git behavior in unborn-HEAD repos (non-zero exit/status and stderr patterns), an OS/subprocess boundary. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior token "repository that has no commits yet" triggers real git process error modes; rejected Agentic/Workflow due no AI pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new temp repo with `git init` and no commits, run CLI subprocess, assert success output is an empty report (all zero/empty sections) and no traceback/error.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real git history traversal, not just local counting logic. Real commit graph/diff behavior is required to expose mismatches. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior token "actual change history of the repository" requires real repo + git subprocess semantics. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no LLM decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across multiple commits, run CLI subprocess, and assert reported category totals exactly equal expected history-derived totals.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real git index vs working-tree state transitions and CLI invocation over actual repo state; mocks can hide index semantics bugs. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior token "staged or unstaged" is specifically an external git state boundary. Rejected Agentic/Workflow since there is no AI/ML step.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, make equivalent edits in two scenarios (staged-only vs unstaged-only), run real CLI subprocess in each scenario, normalize volatile fields, and assert outputs are identical.
